turnaround_reason: Largest standalone behavioral health provider in the US, stock down ~60% from highs after a $1B goodwill impairment, DOJ criminal investigation, class-action lawsuits, and complete executive turnover. However, recent Q1 2026 earnings crushed estimates (EPS $0.37 vs $0.26 est, +39.7% surprise), revenue growth is 7.6%, and the behavioral health demand tailwind is structural with utilization up 60%+ since 2018.
bullish_points: Massive EPS beat Q1 2026 ($0.37 vs $0.26 est, +39.7%) and Q4 2025 ($0.07 vs $0.01 est, +593%) — operational recovery underway
Structural demand tailwind: behavioral health utilization up 60%+ from 2018-2024, accelerating post-COVID
9% bed capacity expansion provides organic growth runway
EV/Revenue of 1.5x and P/B of 1.18x are dirt cheap for a healthcare services company
14 analysts with Buy consensus and mean target $28.86 (+13% upside)
bearish_points: DOJ criminal investigation and class-action lawsuits create existential legal risk
Negative profit margins (-32.8%) driven by $1B goodwill impairment — underlying profitability unclear
Negative FCF (-$217M) — burning cash despite positive operating cash flow
38.6% short float — massive short interest signals serious bear case
$2.7B debt load with only $158M cash and current ratio 1.7 — leverage constrains flexibility
catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (Jul 29, 2026) — continued EPS beats + progress on DOJ settlement would validate the turnaround thesis and force short covering
suggested_trade: Jan 2027 $25 calls or call spread $25/$35 sized small — binary catalyst with DOJ overhang but strong operational momentum
key_risk: DOJ investigation escalates to criminal charges or massive settlement ($550M+), wiping out equity value or forcing asset sales
would_skip_if: DOJ announces expanded charges or settlement exceeds $700M, or Q2 earnings show margin deterioration
